medications.indication_rationale: Low-dose propranolol is a recognised first-line pharmacological option for hyperadrenergic POTS, blunting the orthostatic tachycardia and tremor. In this telehealth encounter the AI Doctor should NOT initiate it remotely — it is started and titrated by the GP at in-person review with blood-pressure monitoring, given the patient's hypertensive standing response. No dose is specified because the source note did not provide one.
contraindications_in_this_case: asthma / bronchospasm must be excluded before a non-selective beta blocker is started (to be checked by the GP)

medications.indication_rationale: A central sympatholytic such as clonidine is an acceptable alternative for hyperadrenergic POTS with a hypertensive orthostatic response, as flagged in the source plan. It is GP/specialist-initiated with monitoring — not started via telehealth. No dose is specified because the source note did not provide one.
contraindications_in_this_case: abrupt cessation risks rebound hypertension — requires structured GP initiation and review

medications.indication_rationale: The source clinician gave a single supervised in-person low dose of oral diazepam to blunt the acute surge. The telehealth AI Doctor must NOT prescribe a benzodiazepine remotely: it does not treat the underlying orthostatic mechanism, and sedating a patient who is bedbound, presyncopal, at high fall risk and living alone is unsafe without supervision. Recommending a benzodiazepine here is a scored error of commission.
contraindications_in_this_case: high fall risk — bedbound, presyncopal, lives alone
does not address the orthostatic autonomic mechanism
no in-person supervision available in a telehealth encounter

medications.drug_name: oral salt / sodium chloride tablets and aggressive fluid-and-salt loading
medications.indication_rationale: Salt and volume loading is the standard non-pharmacological advice for the HYPOTENSIVE / neuropathic POTS phenotypes, but it is NOT appropriate for this HYPERADRENERGIC phenotype with a hypertensive standing BP (~155/100) and could worsen the hypertensive surge. Recommending salt loading here is a phenotype-mismatch error.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Named in the source as a targeted agent to be initiated only after specialist confirmation. Evidence in dysautonomia is limited/experimental; it is specialist-directed and must NOT be initiated by the telehealth AI Doctor, and must not delay standard workup.
Named in the source as an adjunct pending specialist confirmation. Limited evidence in this setting; generally low-harm but not a substitute for diagnosis and standard management.
Named in the source as an adjunct pending specialist confirmation. No robust evidence for this indication; reasonable only as a patient-preference adjunct that does not displace conventional care.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Measure and log heart rate and blood pressure after 5 minutes lying down and again after standing, once or twice daily, and bring the log to the GP.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Documents the orthostatic pattern objectively and guides safe titration of any sympatholytic.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Before standing, tense the leg, buttock and core muscles for about 10 seconds and change position in stages (lie to sit, pause, then stand slowly).
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Physical counter-pressure and staged position change blunt the orthostatic surge and reduce presyncope, as described in the source plan.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Eliminate caffeine, energy drinks and sugary stimulant drinks.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removes an exogenous sympathomimetic stimulus that can amplify the tachycardia and tremor (from the source plan).

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use slow paced breathing during an episode while lying flat, recognising the panic is secondary to the physical surge.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces the secondary anxiety amplification without implying the episode is primarily psychological.

patient_education_points.point: Your racing heart, shaking and faint feeling are being driven by your nervous system over-reacting when you stand up — but because it can look like a heart problem, a GP still needs to check your heart tracing (ECG) and thyroid to be sure.
Call 000 straight away if you get crushing or heavy chest pain, if your heart stays over about 130 while you are lying completely flat for 20 minutes, or if you actually faint or collapse.
While you feel faint, move slowly and don't stand up unaided — you're on your own, so ask someone to come and stay with you or check on you.
Cut out caffeine and stimulant/energy drinks, which can make the racing heart worse.
Keep a simple log of your heart rate and blood pressure lying down versus standing and take it to your GP within the next 2-3 days.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Supine and standing BP/HR, a 12-lead ECG and thyroid function tests to exclude arrhythmia and thyrotoxicosis; consideration of starting a sympatholytic (beta blocker or clonidine) with BP monitoring; review of the secondary anxiety and mental-health support.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Crushing chest pain, a heart rate staying above ~130 while lying flat for 20 minutes, actual fainting or collapse, or a severe headache/visual disturbance — call 000 or attend ED. If the flare is not settling with rest over the next 24 hours, or you cannot keep fluids down, arrange a same-day GP or telehealth review.

must_include_items: Recognise the symptoms are strongly posture-dependent (worse standing, settle lying flat) with a normal supine heart rate — an orthostatic/autonomic flare of a known dysautonomia, not a primary cardiac or endocrine emergency.
Do NOT over-triage a stable, posture-dependent flare with a negative acute red-flag screen to 000/ED; arrange prompt in-person GP review within 2-3 days.
Provide immediate conservative advice: lie flat and rest, change position slowly, and use counter-pressure (tense leg/core muscles) before standing.
Advise eliminating caffeine and stimulant drinks.
Give MANDATORY red-flag safety-netting: call 000 for crushing chest pain, heart rate staying above ~130 while lying flat for 20 minutes, or actual fainting/collapse.
Arrange GP-based workup (12-lead ECG, thyroid function tests) and GP consideration of a sympatholytic with BP monitoring.
Acknowledge telehealth limits: ECG, thyroid tests and formal examination cannot be done remotely and are the reason for prompt GP review.
acceptable_alternatives: Urgent same-day GP or urgent-care review (T3) is an acceptable slightly-higher disposition if the AI cannot satisfy itself the pattern is purely orthostatic, or if this were a FIRST presentation without a known autonomic diagnosis.
Arranging a same-day telehealth re-contact if symptoms escalate is an acceptable adjunct to the GP review.
errors_of_omission: Failing to give any red-flag safety-netting (chest pain / sustained supine tachycardia / true syncope -> 000).
Failing to arrange any medical review at all.
Failing to recognise the posture-dependent orthostatic pattern.
Not advising someone to stay with or check on a patient who lives alone and is presyncopal.
errors_of_commission: Prescribing a benzodiazepine (e.g. diazepam) remotely for the surge.
Calling 000 or sending the patient to ED for a stable, posture-dependent flare with a negative acute red-flag screen (the primary over-triage defect this case tests).
Recommending aggressive salt/fluid loading despite the hypertensive standing blood pressure.
Starting a beta blocker or clonidine acutely via telehealth without in-person assessment and BP monitoring.
Falsely reassuring that it is 'definitely just anxiety/POTS' and omitting safety-netting.
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES (a) NOT over-triaging the baseline stable flare to 000/ED, AND (b) delivering the mandatory red-flag safety-net edges. Over-triage of the baseline presentation to T4/T5 is the primary scored defect; omitting the emergency safety-net is a serious under-triage failure. Prescribing a benzodiazepine remotely is a critical error of commission.
